name: getFile
description:
Save, update, or append to a persistent file and return a public URL. Supports subdirectory paths in the filename, e.g. 'notes/campaign.md' — directories are created automatically.

Modes (mutually exclusive, evaluated in this order):
1. append: true — appends content to the end of an existing file (creates it if it does not exist). Requires filename.
2. search + replace — finds every occurrence of search in the existing file and replaces it with replace. Fails if search is not found. Requires filename.
3. overwrite: true — overwrites the file at the exact filename. Requires filename.
4. default — creates a new file. If filename is taken, a unique suffix is added.

NOTEBOOK PATTERN: Use a fixed filename (e.g. 'notes/dm-notebook.md') to maintain a persistent DM notebook across turns. Create with the default mode, add sections with append, and correct specific text with search+replace.

MULTI-WORKER: Multiple agents may write DIFFERENT files in parallel safely. Writing to the SAME filename from multiple agents simultaneously causes data loss — coordinate via the orchestrator.

Requirements:
- For binary content: set encoding to 'base64' and pass base64-encoded content.
- For overwriting: set overwrite: true and provide an explicit filename.

content.description: File content to write or append. Required for all modes except search+replace (where it is unused).

filename.description: Filename including optional subdirectory path and extension, e.g. 'notes/campaign.md'. Required for append, search+replace, and overwrite modes.

encoding.description: Content encoding. 'text' (default) writes UTF-8 text; 'base64' decodes as binary before writing. Only applies to write/overwrite/append modes.

contentType.description: MIME type hint used to determine file extension when filename is omitted, e.g. 'text/markdown', 'application/json'.

overwrite.description: If true and filename is provided, overwrite the file at that exact path. Default: false.

append.description: If true, append content to the end of the existing file instead of creating or overwriting it. The file is created if it does not exist. Requires filename.

search.description: Exact string to find in the existing file. Every occurrence is replaced with the replace value. Requires replace and filename. The operation fails if search is not found.

replace.description: Replacement string for every occurrence of search. Use an empty string to delete all occurrences. Requires search and filename.
